label: Homicides Since Sep 9, 2024
contestNote: Official SNSP data undercounts; Noroeste field tally is higher. March 2026 was the lowest monthly figure since September 2024 (121 homicides) — a meaningful deceleration. Includes Apr 16: 5 Chapitos arrested Escuinapa; Apr 20: US visa restrictions on 75 cartel associates; Apr 21: CIA officers killed in Chihuahua drug lab operation; Apr 22: Major federal operation targets El Guano in Golden Triangle (SEDENA confirms El Guano evaded Apr 23); Apr 23: 10 Gente del Guano including right-hand man 'Abel N' arrested in Tamazula; Apr 24: US Treasury OFAC sanctions 23-person global fentanyl precursor network (India/Guatemala/Mexico). Sinaloa conflict entering a reduced-violence phase per analytical consensus.
deltaNote: March 2026 saw 121 homicides — lowest monthly figure since Sep 2024. January 2026: 159, February: 146, March: 121. Overall trajectory clearly downward from 2025 peak.

label: Families Displaced
contestNote: Official Sebides (state welfare) count as of March 31, 2026: ~3,800 families — nearly doubled from Oct 2025's 1,763. Academic estimates (UAS) remain 9,000+. At least 50 rural communities abandoned.
deltaNote: Official displacement count nearly doubled Oct 2025–Mar 2026 (1,763 to 3,800 families); academic estimates higher still

label: Persons Disappeared
contestNote: Disappearances are the primary lethal mechanism; many bodies never recovered

label: La Mayiza Territory Control
contestNote: Chapitos dispute figure; Culiacán city contested. 'Mayito Flaco' positioned to lead reconstituted Sinaloa Cartel.
deltaNote: Consolidated from ~70% in mid-2025; 'Mayito Flaco' (Ismael Zambada García's son) leading reconstituted cartel. Shift may reduce fentanyl production in favor of cocaine/heroin.

label: US Fentanyl OD Deaths (2024)
contestNote: Down sharply from 76,282 in 2023; multiple causal factors debated
